claims.claim: Czechia total data center market size 152.67 MW in 2025, forecast to reach 183.47 MW by 2030 at 3.74% CAGR
claims.sourceTitle: Czechia Data Center Market Size, Share & 2031 Trends Report
claims.excerpt: The Czechia data center market size stood at 152.67 MW in 2025 and is forecast to reach 183.47 MW by 2030, expanding at a 3.74% CAGR.

claims.sourceTitle: Hungary Data Center Market Size, Share & 2031 Trends Report
claims.excerpt: Base Year Market Size (2025) | 30.81 Thousand megawatt

claims.claim: Hungary data center market expected to grow from 30.81 MW (2025) to 32.26 MW (2026) and reach 40.71 MW by 2031 at 4.72% CAGR
claims.sourceTitle: Hungary Data Center Market Size, Share & 2031 Trends Report
claims.excerpt: The Hungary data center market size is expected to increase from 30.81 MW in 2025 to 32.26 MW in 2026 and reach 40.71 MW by 2031, growing at a CAGR of 4.72% over 2026-2031.

claims.claim: Romania data center market estimated at 77.83 MW in 2025, growing to 93.34 MW in 2026 and 231.64 MW by 2031 at 19.93% CAGR
claims.sourceTitle: Romania Data Center Market Size, Share & 2031 Trends Report
claims.excerpt: Romania Data Center market size in 2026 is estimated at 93.34 MW, growing from 2025 value of 77.83 MW with 2031 projections showing 231.64 MW, growing at 19.93% CAGR over 2026-2031.

claims.claim: Romania accounts for over 40% of upcoming rack capacity in the CEE region
claims.sourceTitle: Central & Eastern Europe Colocation Data Center Portfolio Report 2025-2029
claims.excerpt: Romania leads the CEE region in data center expansion, accounting for over 40% of upcoming rack capacity.

claims.claim: Poland holds close to 50% of CEE data centre space as of end 2022; the seven CEE countries together hold below 6% of total European (17-country) DC space
claims.sourceTitle: Central and Eastern Europe - Data Centre Landscapes - 2022 to 2026
claims.excerpt: Poland remains the largest market in the CEE region, for close to 50% of the DC space as of the end of 2022... The DC space in the seven CEE countries is below 6% of the total space in Europe (17 countries).

claims.claim: Czechia recorded data center market revenue of over $1.4 billion in 2024, ranking third in CEE after Russia and Poland
claims.sourceTitle: CEE: number of data centers by country 2025| Statista
claims.excerpt: Czechia followed in the ranking with a 1.4-billion-dollar revenue.

claims.claim: Czech Republic currently operates approximately 55 data centres, including 27 in Prague
claims.sourceTitle: Czech market sees emerging role in data centres development
claims.excerpt: The Czech Republic currently operates approximately 55 data centres, including 27 in Prague.

claims.claim: 2,870 MW of new data centre capacity was under construction in the EMEA region at the start of 2025, with 64% already pre-leased
claims.sourceTitle: Data centre capacity in the Czech Republic could double by the end of the decade
claims.excerpt: 2,870 megawatts of new data centre capacity was under construction in the Europe, Middle East and Africa region at the start of 2025. Almost 64 percent of this capacity is already pre-leased, mostly to cloud providers and increasingly to artificial intelligence firms.

claims.claim: CRA's Prague Gateway DC construction begun; first 700-rack building due live by end of 2027, part of a planned 26MW site
claims.sourceTitle: Czech telco CRA breaks ground on Prague data center - DCD
claims.excerpt: Company starts work on first phase of 26MW site... Construction on the Prague Gateway DC has begun. The first 700-rack building is due to go live by the end of 2027.

claims.claim: CRA's Tower DC in Prague's Žižkov district expanded by 1.3MW and 171 racks, taking the facility to 620 racks total
claims.sourceTitle: CRA to expand data center outside Prague, Czechia - DCD
claims.excerpt: The company this week announced it is growing the Tower data center in Prague's Žižkov district, adding an additional 1.3MW of capacity and 171 racks. The CZK 200 million ($8.4m) expansion will take the facility to 620 racks.

claims.claim: Arizton CEE report covers 341 data center facilities (290 existing, 51 upcoming) across 4 key countries including Austria, Czech Republic, Poland
claims.sourceTitle: Central & Eastern Europe Data Centers: Existing & Upcoming Data Centers
claims.excerpt: Discover Insights on 341 Data Center Facilities Across 4 Key Countries in Central & Eastern Europe... 290 Existing Data Centers 51 Upcoming Data Centers 150 Data Center Operators 4 No. of Cities

claims.claim: CEE Data Centre Landscapes report covers seven countries (Bulgaria, Czechia, Hungary, Poland, Romania, Slovakia, Slovenia) with close to 200 data centre facilities in the region
claims.sourceTitle: Central and Eastern Europe - Data Centre Landscapes - 2022 to 2026
claims.excerpt: There are close to 200 Data Centre Facilities in the CEE region.

claims.claim: As of end 2022, Hungary had over 30,000 m2 of third-party Data Centre raised floor space and was described as a larger market than Romania but significantly smaller than Czechia
claims.excerpt: Hungary is a significant market in the CEE (Central Eastern Europe) region, with over 30,000 m2 of third-party Data Centre raised floor space available as of the end of 2022 and is a larger market than Romania but is significantly smaller than the Czechia market.

claims.claim: Czech data centre market is primarily constrained by limited available power capacity, absence of major hyperscale players, and lengthy permitting processes
claims.sourceTitle: Czech market sees emerging role in data centres development
claims.excerpt: Today, the Czech data centre market is primarily constrained by limited available power capacity, the absence of major hyperscale players, and lengthy permitting processes.

tensions.text: Mordor's Romania base-year figure (77.83 MW in 2025, growing to 231.64 MW by 2031) is roughly 2.9x the Statista colocation-only figure (27 MW) used to build geo.other's residual bucket — the two sources appear to measure different scopes (total market vs colocation) with no reconciliation in the node.

Mordor's Hungary base-year figure is listed as '30.81 Thousand megawatt' (2025) — an apparent unit/data anomaly — yet even at face value (30.81 MW) it is ~1.7x the Statista colocation figure (18 MW) feeding geo.other, raising doubt about cross-source comparability.

An older 2022 Hungary landscape report states Hungary is 'a larger market than Romania,' directly contradicting the newer Statista 2025 breakdown (Hungary 18 MW < Romania 27 MW) that geo.other's residual bucket relies on.

Mordor's Czechia figure (152.67 MW, 2025) appears to be a total data-center market estimate (all segments), while geo.CZ's derivation uses a colocation-only subset (79 MW colo ÷ 3,451 MW CE-7 colo) — the node's 'cross-check' comparison mixes total-market and colocation-only metrics without flagging the scope difference.

absences: No source in this batch supplied country-level colocation MW figures for Slovakia, Slovenia, Croatia, or Bulgaria — the Statista breakdown used in geo.other's derivation was not independently corroborated by this search group.
No source verified the CE-7 colocation total denominator of 3,451 MW cited in both geo.CZ and geo.other derivations.
No breakdown was found isolating Czechia's colocation-specific capacity (the 79 MW figure used in geo.CZ) from its total data-center market of 152.67 MW reported by Mordor.

No Poland-specific installed capacity (MW) figure was found despite Poland being cited as holding ~50% of CEE data-centre space, leaving the CE-7 proportional math unverifiable against a primary Poland number.
No figure was found translating Romania's '40% of upcoming CEE rack capacity' claim into an installed-MW equivalent, leaving inconsistency with Mordor's Romania MW trajectory unresolved.
